subject: ap-psychology
title: Research Methods & Experimental Design
stimulus:
Rayce is exploring the idea of using online videos to sell his line of custom-made skateboards. W hile his friend
is making a video of him performing tricks at a local skate park, a large crowd forms to watch. Each time
Rayce successfully completes a trick, the crowd loudly applauds and yells, showing their approval.

parts.prompt: Explain how Vestibular sense relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: The response must indicate that this sense influences Rayce’s skateboarding by providing a sense of balance (e.g., equilibrium, information about position of the head in relation to gravity). OR The response must indicate that disruption of this sense leads to dizziness.
Acceptable: Rayce’s vestibular sense allows him to maintain balance as he’s performing tricks.; Rayce becomes dizzy doing the stunts because his vestibular sense is out of whack.

parts.prompt: Explain how Social facilitation relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: The response must indicate that Rayce will perform his tricks better if there is an audience present. OR The response must indicate Rayce will perform less well at a certain trick if he is not very good at that trick if an audience is present.
Acceptable: After the crowd gathers, his jumps are higher, and his turns are tighter because of; Because he is doing a fairly new trick the presence of a crowd impedes his

parts.prompt: Explain how Incentive theory relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: The response must indicate that Rayce is motivated to perform because he is receiving some external reward (e.g., money, praise, applause, cheering, approval).
Acceptable: Rayce rides his skateboard for the crowd because he wants the applause

parts.prompt: Later, Rayce puts the video of himself online, complete with bright graphics and fast-tempo music. He quickly gains new customers for his business by allowing people to try his skateboard free for an hour. Explain how Divergent thinking relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: The response must indicate Rayce uses or considers many possible approaches to the campaign. OR The response must refer to divergent thinking as “thinking outside the box,” innovation, or creativity.
Acceptable: Rayce used multiple approaches to his campaign with both a video and trying the; Rayce came up with innovative ideas like the bright graphics.

parts.prompt: Later, Rayce puts the video of himself online, complete with bright graphics and fast-tempo music. He quickly gains new customers for his business by allowing people to try his skateboard free for an hour. Explain how Peripheral route to persuasion relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: The response must indicate the campaign used attention-grabbing elements.
Acceptable: Rayce uses graphics and music in his video to persuade people to buy his; Customers are persuaded to buy the boards by the cool soundtrack in the video.

parts.prompt: Later, Rayce puts the video of himself online, complete with bright graphics and fast-tempo music. He quickly gains new customers for his business by allowing people to try his skateboard free for an hour. Explain how Reciprocity norm relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: The response must indicate that because potential customers are getting something from Rayce (e.g., one-hour free trial, a sticker, cool hat), they feel obliged to return the favor by making a purchase or promoting the product.
Acceptable: Because Rayce allowed them to try the skateboard for free, customers returned

parts.prompt: Later, Rayce puts the video of himself online, complete with bright graphics and fast-tempo music. He quickly gains new customers for his business by allowing people to try his skateboard free for an hour. Explain how Mere-exposure effect relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: The response must indicate repeated experience to any element of the ad campaign leads to increased liking or purchasing.
Acceptable: The more often that people see the video the more likely they are to buy the
